edges.description: Loss of DDX11 function destabilizes cohesion and increases chromosomal breakage.
edges.target: Sister chromatid cohesion defect and chromosomal instability

edges.description: Cohesion and replication stress perturb higher-order chromatin organization.

edges.description: Replication stress and altered transcription disturb nucleolar ribosome production.

edges.description: Replication-stress and chromatin-organization defects contribute to growth failure.

edges.description: Developmental impairment contributes to reduced head growth.

edges.description: Developmental disturbance contributes to inner-ear dysfunction and hearing loss.

edges.description: Developmental patterning defects contribute to facial dysmorphism.

edges.description: Some patients develop congenital heart defects.

edges.description: Impaired developmental proliferation contributes to growth retardation.
Developmental gene-expression defects contribute to delayed milestones.
Developmental chromatin defects contribute to cognitive impairment.
Impaired developmental proliferation contributes to reduced brain growth.
Reduced ribosome production impairs developmental proliferation.
Ribosome biogenesis defects contribute to delayed developmental milestones.
Ribosome biogenesis defects can impair neurodevelopment.
Reduced developmental proliferation contributes to diminished brain growth.

nodes.description: Facial dysmorphism is repeatedly reported in affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular and Cellular Functions of the Warsaw Breakage Syndrome DNA Helicase DDX11.
evidence.snippet: WABS patients display several clinical features, including severe pre- and post-natal growth retardation, microcephaly, sensorineural hearing loss, cochlear anomalies, facial dysmorphy, abnormal skin pigmentation, cardiac defects, and intellectual disability.

nodes.description: Congenital cardiac defects have been reported in the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular and Cellular Functions of the Warsaw Breakage Syndrome DNA Helicase DDX11.
evidence.snippet: WABS patients display several clinical features, including severe pre- and post-natal growth retardation, microcephaly, sensorineural hearing loss, cochlear anomalies, facial dysmorphy, abnormal skin pigmentation, cardiac defects, and intellectual disability.

evidence.reference_title: Role of the DDX11 DNA Helicase in Warsaw Breakage Syndrome Etiology.
evidence.snippet: WABS is due to biallelic mutations of the gene coding for the super-family 2 DNA helicase DDX11/ChlR1, orthologous to the yeast chromosome loss protein 1 (Chl1).
DDX11 | HGNC:2736 | Warsaw breakage syndrome | MONDO:0013252 | AR | Definitive

nodes.description: Biallelic DDX11 variants impair helicase function at the interface of DNA replication, repair, and sister chromatid cohesion.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular and Cellular Functions of the Warsaw Breakage Syndrome DNA Helicase DDX11.
evidence.snippet: DDX11/ChlR1 (Chl1 in yeast) is a DNA helicase involved in sister chromatid cohesion and in DNA repair pathways.

evidence.reference_title: Role of the DDX11 DNA Helicase in Warsaw Breakage Syndrome Etiology.
evidence.snippet: Warsaw breakage syndrome (WABS) is a genetic disorder characterized by sister chromatid cohesion defects, growth retardation, microcephaly, hearing loss and other variable clinical manifestations.

nodes.description: Developmental delay is a recurring presentation in affected children.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular and Cellular Functions of the Warsaw Breakage Syndrome DNA Helicase DDX11.
evidence.snippet: However, the partial overlap of clinical manifestations (growth retardation, microcephaly, intellectual disability) with other cohesinopathies (CdLS and RBS) suggests that all these diseases share common developmental defects due to altered transcription profiles during embryonic development.

nodes.description: Severe pre- and postnatal growth retardation is a cardinal feature.

evidence.reference_title: Role of the DDX11 DNA Helicase in Warsaw Breakage Syndrome Etiology.
evidence.snippet: Warsaw breakage syndrome (WABS) is a genetic disorder characterized by sister chromatid cohesion defects, growth retardation, microcephaly, hearing loss and other variable clinical manifestations.

nodes.description: DDX11 contributes to heterochromatin maintenance, so loss of function perturbs chromatin architecture.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular and Cellular Functions of the Warsaw Breakage Syndrome DNA Helicase DDX11.
evidence.snippet: Moreover, DDX11 was believed to contribute to heterochromatin formation by targeting HP1α factor to proper sites in pericentric regions and at telomeres in DDX11-depleted HeLa and in DDX11 knockout mouse embryo-derived cells [67].

evidence.reference_title: Molecular and Cellular Functions of the Warsaw Breakage Syndrome DNA Helicase DDX11.
evidence.snippet: WABS patients display several clinical features, including severe pre- and post-natal growth retardation, microcephaly, sensorineural hearing loss, cochlear anomalies, facial dysmorphy, abnormal skin pigmentation, cardiac defects, and intellectual disability.

nodes.description: Intellectual disability occurs in the broader syndrome spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular and Cellular Functions of the Warsaw Breakage Syndrome DNA Helicase DDX11.
evidence.snippet: However, the partial overlap of clinical manifestations (growth retardation, microcephaly, intellectual disability) with other cohesinopathies (CdLS and RBS) suggests that all these diseases share common developmental defects due to altered transcription profiles during embryonic development.

nodes.description: Microcephaly is one of the core and most consistent findings.

evidence.reference_title: Role of the DDX11 DNA Helicase in Warsaw Breakage Syndrome Etiology.
evidence.snippet: Warsaw breakage syndrome (WABS) is a genetic disorder characterized by sister chromatid cohesion defects, growth retardation, microcephaly, hearing loss and other variable clinical manifestations.

nodes.description: DDX11 down-regulation suppresses ribosomal RNA synthesis and reduces cell proliferation, indicating a ribosome biogenesis defect.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular and Cellular Functions of the Warsaw Breakage Syndrome DNA Helicase DDX11.
evidence.snippet: In addition, it was recently found that DDX11 localizes at the nucleolus in HeLa cells and preferentially binds to hypomethylated active ribosomal DNA gene loci, where it interacts with the RNA polymerase I transcriptional machinery. DDX11 down-regulation caused suppression of ribosomal RNA synthesis, inhibiting cell proliferation [68].

nodes.description: Sensorineural hearing loss is part of the recurrent clinical spectrum.

evidence.snippet: Warsaw breakage syndrome (WABS) is a genetic disorder characterized by sister chromatid cohesion defects, growth retardation, microcephaly, hearing loss and other variable clinical manifestations.

nodes.description: DDX11 loss causes premature chromatid separation, chromosomal breakage, and broader chromosomal instability in patient cells.
